title: Don’t let AI steal all the joy: what scientists won’t give up to chatbots
excerpt: Nature asked readers of the Nature Briefing what they like about their job and don’t want to hand over.
content_html:
With artificial-intelligence tools speeding up and taking on ever more steps in science, Nature canvassed opinions on tasks that readers would rather keep for themselves. Here is a snapshot of the responses.

Keep asking questions

As a student involved in research into AI and consciousness, the most exciting part is being curious, noticing unexpected connections and challenging existing assumptions. These moments often come from conversations with mentors, discussions with colleagues and personal reflection rather than from an algorithm. — Müleyke Özçakmak Istanbul, Turkey.

The buck stops here

As a deep-sea scientist, it would be difficult to pass over my inspiration, the thrill of discovery and the creativity to design experiments. A personal connection to the deep-ocean realm is required for conservation, and I have an ethical responsibility in science. — Elva Escobar Mexico City, Mexico.

Let me clean up my data myself

I have a background in biostatistics and I enjoy statistical programming. Getting messy data and making them all neat and presentable is such a pleasure. — Lucy Njoki Njuki Manchester, UK.

AI’s mistakes take too long to fix

Although AI’s efficiency at writing code makes me green with envy, I still prefer writing my own code. I love this part of my job. Code written by AI can be difficult and time-consuming to debug. — Richard Kunz Pietermaritzburg, South Africa.

Chats with colleagues are crucial

Scientific progress is not only about finding answers efficiently but also about exchanging perspectives, challenging assumptions and developing ideas through conversation. Many of my best research ideas emerge from spontaneous discussions. — Sheng Bao Hong Kong, China.

If AI does it better, it should do it

As a doctor and health-care researcher, I must always put the interest of the patient first. So if AI does any task better than me, it should replace me on that task. That is scary, but my hope is that AI will augment and not replace my calling to care for others, my human touch, my curiosity-driven research, the fun of co-creating with other people and the moral compass for health-equity improvement. — Enrique Falceto De Barros Porto Alegre, Brazil.

Writing is thinking

I’m not interested in having chatbots take over my writing and reviewing tasks. Writing helps me to realize what aspects I still don’t understand. Reviewing others’ work lets me put on my critical-thinking hat. Even writing boring e-mails helps me to remember to work on strong and effective communication skills. — Anna Hodshire Fort Collins, Colorado.

An interview needs a person

I prefer to handle primary data collection, on-site observation and in-person interviews myself. This includes the use of in-depth questionnaires and fieldwork surveys, and speaking to people receiving medical treatment. These tasks demand empathy, flexible, on-the-spot responses and professional face-to-face communication. AI cannot access real-world scenarios to gather authentic first-hand materials, so I will never leave this part to algorithms. — Liao Mengchao Beijing, China.

Tackling graphics is satisfying

sentences.zh: 作为一名医生和医疗保健研究人员，我必须始终将患者的利益放在第一位。

sentences.zh: 因此，如果人工智能在任何任务上比我做得更好，它应该取代我来完成该任务。

sentences.en: That is scary, but my hope is that AI will augment and not replace my calling to care for others, my human touch, my curiosity-driven research, the fun of co-creating with other people and the moral compass for health-equity improvement. — Enrique Falceto De Barros Porto Alegre, Brazil.
sentences.zh: 这很可怕，但我希望人工智能能够增强而不是取代我关心他人的呼唤、人性化的接触、好奇心驱动的研究、与他人共同创造的乐趣以及改善健康公平的道德指南针。 — 恩里克·法尔塞托·德巴罗斯 阿雷格里港，巴西。

sentences.en: Writing is thinking
sentences.zh: 写作就是思考

sentences.en: Writing helps me to realize what aspects I still don’t understand.
sentences.zh: 写作帮助我认识到哪些方面我仍然不明白。

sentences.en: Reviewing others’ work lets me put on my critical-thinking hat.
sentences.zh: 回顾别人的工作让我戴上批判性思维的帽子。

sentences.en: Even writing boring e-mails helps me to remember to work on strong and effective communication skills. — Anna Hodshire Fort Collins, Colorado.
sentences.zh: 即使写无聊的电子邮件也能帮助我记住加强有效的沟通技巧。 — 安娜·霍德郡，科罗拉多州柯林斯堡。

sentences.en: An interview needs a person
sentences.zh: 面试需要一个人

blocks.html_en: I prefer to handle primary data collection, on-site observation and in-person interviews myself. This includes the use of in-depth questionnaires and fieldwork surveys, and speaking to people receiving medical treatment. These tasks demand empathy, flexible, on-the-spot responses and professional face-to-face communication. AI cannot access real-world scenarios to gather authentic first-hand materials, so I will never leave this part to algorithms. — Liao Mengchao Beijing, China.

sentences.en: I prefer to handle primary data collection, on-site observation and in-person interviews myself.
sentences.zh: 我更喜欢自己处理主要数据收集、现场观察和面对面访谈。

sentences.en: This includes the use of in-depth questionnaires and fieldwork surveys, and speaking to people receiving medical treatment.
sentences.zh: 这包括使用深入的问卷和实地调查，以及与接受治疗的人交谈。

sentences.en: These tasks demand empathy, flexible, on-the-spot responses and professional face-to-face communication.
sentences.zh: 这些任务需要同理心、灵活的现场反应和专业的面对面沟通。

sentences.en: AI cannot access real-world scenarios to gather authentic first-hand materials, so I will never leave this part to algorithms. — Liao Mengchao Beijing, China.
sentences.zh: AI无法接触现实世界的场景来收集真实的第一手资料，所以我永远不会把这部分交给算法。 — 廖孟超 中国北京。

sentences.en: Tackling graphics is satisfying
sentences.zh: 处理图形是令人满意的

content_html_zh:
随着人工智能工具的加速发展并在科学领域迈出越来越多的步伐，《自然》杂志就读者宁愿自己保留的任务征求了意见。 这是响应的快照。

继续提问

作为一名参与人工智能和意识研究的学生，最令人兴奋的部分是保持好奇心，注意到意想不到的联系并挑战现有的假设。 这些时刻通常来自与导师的对话、与同事的讨论以及个人反思，而不是来自算法。 — Müleyke Özçakmak 土耳其伊斯坦布尔。

责任到此为止

作为一名深海科学家，我很难忽视我的灵感、发现的兴奋和设计实验的创造力。 保护工作需要与深海领域的个人联系，而且我对科学负有道德责任。 — 埃尔瓦·埃斯科瓦尔 墨西哥，墨西哥城。

让我自己清理我的数据

我有生物统计学背景，并且喜欢统计编程。 获取凌乱的数据并将它们整理得井井有条是一种乐趣。 — Lucy Nj​​oki Njuki 英国曼彻斯特。

人工智能的错误需要很长时间才能修复

虽然AI写代码的效率让我羡慕不已，但我还是更喜欢自己写代码。 我喜欢我工作的这一部分。 人工智能编写的代码调试起来既困难又耗时。 — 理查德·昆兹 彼得马里茨堡，南非。

与同事的聊天很重要

科学进步不仅在于有效地找到答案，还在于通过对话交换观点、挑战假设和发展想法。 我的许多最好的研究想法都来自自发的讨论。 ——盛宝，中国香港。

如果人工智能做得更好，它就应该这样做

作为一名医生和医疗保健研究人员，我必须始终将患者的利益放在第一位。 因此，如果人工智能在任何任务上比我做得更好，它应该取代我来完成该任务。 这很可怕，但我希望人工智能能够增强而不是取代我关心他人的呼唤、人性化的接触、好奇心驱动的研究、与他人共同创造的乐趣以及改善健康公平的道德指南针。 — 恩里克·法尔塞托·德巴罗斯 阿雷格里港，巴西。

写作就是思考

我对让聊天机器人接管我的写作和审阅任务不感兴趣。 写作帮助我认识到哪些方面我仍然不明白。 回顾别人的工作让我戴上批判性思维的帽子。 即使写无聊的电子邮件也能帮助我记住加强有效的沟通技巧。 — 安娜·霍德郡，科罗拉多州柯林斯堡。

面试需要一个人

我更喜欢自己处理主要数据收集、现场观察和面对面访谈。 这包括使用深入的问卷和实地调查，以及与接受治疗的人交谈。 这些任务需要同理心、灵活的现场反应和专业的面对面沟通。 AI无法接触现实世界的场景来收集真实的第一手资料，所以我永远不会把这部分交给算法。 — 廖孟超 中国北京。

处理图形是令人满意的
